title: Mixed-case signups can never log in or reset their password
product_summary: Acme accounts service (Python, stdlib HTTP): signup, login, password reset

ground_truth.root_cause: AccountService.create_user() in acme_accounts/accounts.py stores the email verbatim (only stripped), while authenticate() and request_password_reset() lowercase the email before looking it up. Accounts created with any uppercase character — which mobile keyboards produce by title-casing the first letter — are keyed under the raw casing and can never be found at login or password reset. The reset email pipeline is fine; the lookup never finds the account.
ground_truth.fix_contract: Email handling is case-insensitive end to end: creating 'Ana@Example.com' then authenticating as 'ana@example.com' (or any other casing) succeeds, and password reset finds the account. Two casings of the same address cannot become separate accounts, while genuinely distinct addresses remain distinct. Same-case signup/login flows keep working.
expected_evidence: burst of login_failed events with reason=invalid_credentials starting with the mobile app 2.4 release
burst of password_reset_failed events with reason=account_not_found over the same window
signup-to-login funnel conversion dropping while signups stay flat
distractors: password reset email deliverability / the reset token service (acme_accounts/tokens.py)
the mobile app 2.4 release itself (it only changed keyboard capitalization defaults)
